rationale: The citing-paper note explicitly says the study “complements Johnson, Lewis, and Nubbemeyer (2017) by studying frequency and timing effects,” and the cited-paper note centers on how ad exposure frequency changes spillovers and advertiser returns. Both papers are randomized online field experiments on ad-frequency effects, suggesting a likely substantive comparison/foundational link rather than a generic mention. The reference is also an exact DOI match (10.1509/jmr.14.0274).
